governs: Prospective implementation-unsafety classification and orthogonal review-sensitivity tagging for first-party dependency replacements in the Dependency Sovereignty Program.
The minimum evidence a downstream implementation bead must cite before it can claim that a replacement is safety-eligible.
The fail-closed exception process for replacements that would introduce algorithmic unsafe.
The extra review evidence required for public APIs, persisted or wire formats, security-sensitive parsers, cryptographic formats, concurrency/liveness, platform boundaries, and runtime hot paths.
does_not_govern: Whether a replacement is worthwhile on marginal dependency cost, interoperability, performance, maintenance, or product grounds.
Existing unsafe code or existing rows in artifacts/unsafe_boundary_ledger_v1.json.
Third-party dependency safety in general or the safety of transitive dependency implementations.
Release readiness, broad workspace health, runtime correctness, or performance parity.
policy_scope.no_claim_boundary: This two-axis taxonomy governs prospective dependency-replacement approvals only. It does not retroactively judge, revoke, certify, or reclassify existing ledgered unsafe boundaries. A matching implementation-unsafety class and review-sensitivity tag set are not proof that their evidence requirements or separate program gates have been satisfied.

eligibility_states.eligible: The safety class permits implementation without an exception when the row and its separate program gates are satisfied.
eligibility_states.eligible_with_required_evidence: The safety class permits implementation only after every class evidence item is linked from the implementation bead.
eligibility_states.prohibited: The replacement must not be implemented or approved under the default policy.
eligibility_states.exception_approved: ALGORITHMIC-UNSAFE is permitted only when the complete exception record is present and explicit owner sign-off is linked.

classes.definition: First-party replacement code that is clean under #![forbid(unsafe_code)] and does not rely on first-party unsafe helpers to implement the replacement.
classes.unsafe_scope: No first-party unsafe code is permitted in the replacement surface.
required_evidence: A source or crate-level forbid(unsafe_code) gate, or an equivalent contract test proving the complete replacement surface contains no first-party unsafe code.
Focused unit, differential, and end-to-end evidence required by the owning implementation bead.
UBS coverage for every owned file before approval.
A proof note that names the exact replacement surface and its separate program gates.
review_rules: Do not weaken the class by hiding unsafe behind a new first-party helper or generated file.
Treat retained third-party dependencies and platform libraries as separate trust relationships rather than calling them first-party safe code.
Safety eligibility does not waive semantic parity, deterministic testing, marginal-cost, performance, or interoperability gates.
explicit_no_claims: The absence of first-party unsafe does not prove functional correctness.
SAFE-OWN does not prove performance parity or justify removing the incumbent.
SAFE-OWN does not certify transitive dependencies as safe.

classes.definition: Narrow, ledgered unsafe at an OS/FFI or CPU-dispatch boundary, including syscall wrappers, target-feature dispatch shims, and process-environment mutation.
classes.unsafe_scope: Only the smallest practical boundary item may contain unsafe code; item- or function-scoped #[allow(unsafe_code)] is required when practical.
required_evidence: A written explanation of why a safe-Rust alternative is insufficient for this boundary.
The narrowest practical item- or function-scoped #[allow(unsafe_code)] around the unsafe operation.
A matching row in artifacts/unsafe_boundary_ledger_v1.json with an explicit safety invariant and no-claim boundary.
Category-specific evidence in docs/unsafe_boundary_ledger.md.
Miri coverage for executable unsafe behavior, or a documented toolchain or platform limitation plus an alternate focused proof.
UBS coverage for every owned boundary file.
A target-gated RCH proof note that states which hosts or architectures were not exercised.
review_rules: The implementation bead must link every required evidence item; a category label alone is not approval.
Keep OS handles, raw pointers, target-feature assumptions, and ownership transfer inside the ledgered boundary.
Apply any separate measured-defect, portability, marginal-cost, security-review, or owner-signoff gate recorded on the classification row.
explicit_no_claims: Passing the taxonomy contract does not execute or verify platform FFI.
Evidence from one host or architecture does not prove another host or architecture.
A ledger row is an inventory and review contract, not proof that unsafe code is correct.

classes.definition: Unsafe code that encodes an ownership, liveness, initialization, pinning, or weak-memory argument, including lock-free pointer tagging, MaybeUninit inline storage, futex parking protocols, and generated pin projection.
classes.unsafe_scope: Prohibited for dependency replacements by default; no implementation work may start from a prohibited row.
required_evidence: A complete algorithmic_unsafe_exception_process record is mandatory before eligibility can change to exception_approved.
Partial exception evidence fails closed and leaves the row prohibited.
review_rules: Loom does not prove liveness or linearizability.
Miri does not model weak memory.
A 48-hour soak is not a proof.
Moving algorithmic unsafe from a mature, widely-fuzzed crate into fresh first-party code increases risk.
Prefer retaining the incumbent or benchmarking a SAFE-OWN alternative.
explicit_no_claims: Loom, Miri, fuzzing, or soak evidence alone cannot approve an ALGORITHMIC-UNSAFE replacement.
A performance hypothesis is not a measured incumbent defect.
Taxonomy contract success does not grant an exception.

requirements.measured_incumbent_performance_defect: A reproducible benchmark or production trace must identify a concrete defect in the incumbent on the real Asupersync workload.
requirements.safe_alternative_benchmark: At least one SAFE-OWN alternative must be implemented far enough to measure on the same workload and full gate axes.
requirements.safe_alternative_rejection_rationale: The decision record must explain why the measured SAFE-OWN result is unacceptable and why no narrower safe design satisfies the requirement.
requirements.owner_signoff: The repository owner must explicitly approve the named algorithmic-unsafe replacement after reviewing the defect and safe-alternative evidence.

no_implicit_approval: An open bead, prototype, benchmark plan, or taxonomy classification is not owner sign-off.
Evidence for a mature incumbent cannot be reused as evidence for fresh first-party unsafe code.
No field may be omitted, marked pending, or inferred from another field.

review_sensitivity_tags.definition: No additional sensitivity category applies beyond the implementation-unsafety class and the owning bead's ordinary semantic gates.
required_evidence: Focused semantic tests and the owning bead's normal proof packet.
review_rules: ordinary is exclusive and must not be combined with another review-sensitivity tag.
Do not use ordinary to avoid a more specific tag.
explicit_no_claims: ordinary does not mean trivial, risk-free, or exempt from program gates.

review_sensitivity_tags.definition: The replacement changes a public type, trait, format enum, feature contract, or consumer-visible behavior.
required_evidence: An approved ADR naming every changed public symbol and chosen end-state.
An artifacts/api_surface_map_v1.json update in the implementation commit.
Consumer migration and no-compatibility-shim notes.
review_rules: Treat removal of external interoperability as a product decision, not an internal refactor.
Do not add deprecated aliases, wrappers, or compatibility shims.
explicit_no_claims: A safe implementation does not approve the public API break.

review_sensitivity_tags.definition: The replacement reads or writes bytes that may outlive the producing process or release.
required_evidence: A versioned schema or canonical-format contract.
Golden fixtures covering prior supported encodings and boundary values.
An explicit migration, rejection, or retention policy for existing data.
review_rules: Fail closed on unknown versions and non-canonical encodings.
Never delete legacy fixtures or user data without explicit permission.
explicit_no_claims: Round-trip success does not prove compatibility with historical artifacts.

review_sensitivity_tags.definition: The replacement parses or emits protocol bytes exchanged across a process, host, or trust boundary.
required_evidence: Canonical and adversarial wire vectors, including truncation, length, overflow, and non-canonical cases.
Differential or standards-based fixtures with documented provenance.
A fuzz or exhaustive bounded-input strategy for the decoder.
review_rules: A parser written entirely in safe Rust remains SAFE-OWN; this tag does not imply unsafe code.
Reject malformed and unsupported input deterministically.
explicit_no_claims: Differential agreement does not prove both wire implementations correct.

review_sensitivity_tags.definition: Parser behavior can affect authentication, authorization, confidentiality, integrity, redaction, or resource-exhaustion boundaries.
required_evidence: Independent security review of accepted, rejected, and ambiguous inputs.
Negative fixtures for confusion, truncation, non-canonical, amplification, and bypass cases.
Explicit fail-closed and resource-bound contracts.
review_rules: Classify by security consequence even when every implementation line is safe Rust.
Do not infer acceptance from another parser's permissive behavior.
explicit_no_claims: Memory safety does not prove parser security or denial-of-service resistance.

review_sensitivity_tags.definition: The replacement owns encoding, key-kind, checksum, domain-separation, or canonicalization rules adjacent to retained cryptographic primitives.
required_evidence: Standards or upstream-reference vectors for every supported key or message kind.
Canonical encoding, secret redaction, zeroization, and type-confusion review.
Independent security sign-off before cutover.
review_rules: Do not reimplement cryptographic primitives under this tag.
Unknown key kinds, prefixes, and encodings fail closed.
explicit_no_claims: Wire compatibility does not prove cryptographic soundness.

review_sensitivity_tags.definition: The replacement can affect wakeups, cancellation, fairness, ownership transfer, quiescence, or progress.
required_evidence: Deterministic LabRuntime or DPOR coverage for cancellation and wake races.
No-task-leak, no-obligation-leak, loser-drain, and region-quiescence assertions where applicable.
A liveness argument plus bounded stress evidence.
review_rules: Loom or stress evidence supplements but does not prove liveness or linearizability.
Preserve structured-concurrency and cancel-correctness invariants.
explicit_no_claims: Passing schedules do not exhaust all scheduler or weak-memory behaviors.

review_sensitivity_tags.definition: The replacement depends on OS ABI, target architecture, cache topology, target features, handles, syscalls, or platform-specific semantics.
required_evidence: A target and host matrix naming exercised and unexercised combinations.
Platform documentation or ABI references for every conditional implementation.
Target-gated RCH proof or an explicit no-host limitation receipt.
review_rules: Platform sensitivity is independent of whether the implementation contains unsafe code.
Unknown platform evidence remains blocked rather than being reported safe.
explicit_no_claims: Evidence on one host or architecture does not establish another.

review_sensitivity_tags.definition: The replacement executes on a scheduler, synchronization, allocation, I/O, parsing, or telemetry path where regressions can materially affect runtime behavior.
required_evidence: Before-and-after benchmarks on the owning bead's agreed workload and axes.
Allocation, latency, throughput, or contention measurements appropriate to the path.
A rollback threshold and no-regression interpretation.
review_rules: Do not substitute microbenchmarks for the real runtime workload.
A measured regression blocks cutover unless the owner explicitly changes the gate.
explicit_no_claims: Taxonomy classification contains no benchmark or performance result.

classifications.replacement_surface: Scalar hexadecimal encode and decode helpers.

ordinary: Focused encode/decode semantics, malformed-input, and differential vectors.

program_gates: Differential vectors against the retained incumbent.
Marginal-cost ledger baseline and after measurement.
classifications.notes: Triviality, not graph weight, is the replacement justification.

classifications.replacement_surface: Scalar base64 engines for WebSocket, certificate pin, gRPC-Web, and NATS credential surfaces, including padded URL_SAFE input where the live API requires it.

wire-format-parser: Canonical vectors cover STANDARD, URL_SAFE, padded URL_SAFE, no-pad, whitespace, truncation, and rejected non-canonical forms.
security-sensitive-parser: Independent review covers credential and certificate-pin parsing, input bounds, and fail-closed errors.

program_gates: Differential vectors for every accepted alphabet and padding policy.
Marginal-cost ledger baseline and after measurement.
classifications.notes: The replacement must not reintroduce an unsafe SIMD backend.

classifications.replacement_surface: Owned block_on, poll_fn, zip, race, yield_now, and Future::poll panic-catching adapters used by test and production containment paths.

concurrency-liveness: LabRuntime coverage preserves wake, cancellation, loser-drain, panic-payload, and drop-scope semantics at Future::poll boundaries.
runtime-hot-path: Poll-path overhead is benchmarked for ready, pending, wake, and panic containment cases.

program_gates: Differential behavior against futures-lite as a dev-only oracle.
No second production executor or orphan-task path.
classifications.notes: The implementation must preserve deterministic test behavior and structured concurrency.

classifications.replacement_surface: Generation-tagged in-tree TokenSlab family.

concurrency-liveness: Deterministic ABA, stale-token, removal, and reuse schedules preserve ownership and cancellation invariants.
runtime-hot-path: Allocation, lookup, and removal latency plus memory overhead are measured against slab.

program_gates: ABA and stale-token regression coverage.
Differential allocation and removal behavior.
classifications.notes: Generation tagging is required; an unsafe MaybeUninit slab would be a different class.

classifications.replacement_surface: Owned asupersync-macros visibility attribute.

public-api-redesign: The proc-macro surface map and compile fixtures cover every supported attribute input and emitted visibility.

program_gates: Compile-pass and compile-fail macro fixtures.
Expansion parity for every existing use shape.
classifications.notes: The attribute expansion may not emit unsafe code.

classifications.replacement_surface: Nanosecond timestamp to RFC3339 formatter.

persistent-format: Version-independent RFC3339 goldens cover range limits, leap years, nanoseconds, and historical artifact expectations.

program_gates: Boundary, leap-year, and golden-format vectors.
Behavioral parity at the sole production call site.
classifications.notes: This row covers the owned formatter, not a general time library.

classifications.replacement_surface: Explicit purpose-built snapshot and trace schemas replacing public Bincode format variants.

public-api-redesign: The SerdeCodec ADR and API surface map name the removal of arbitrary Serialize and the Bincode variant.
persistent-format: Versioned snapshot and trace schemas include migration or rejection rules plus historical goldens.
wire-format-parser: Decoder fixtures cover truncation, lengths, unknown versions, non-canonical values, and bounded allocation.

program_gates: Phase-3 public API ADR approval.
Versioned golden and differential corpus.
Migration and compatibility policy for stored artifacts.
classifications.notes: Eligibility does not pre-approve the public API redesign.

classifications.replacement_surface: Explicit purpose-built typed-symbol, snapshot, and trace serialization replacing public MessagePack format variants.

public-api-redesign: The SerdeCodec ADR and API surface map name the removal of arbitrary Serialize and the MessagePack variant.
persistent-format: Versioned typed-symbol, snapshot, and trace schemas include migration or rejection rules plus historical goldens.
wire-format-parser: Decoder fixtures cover truncation, lengths, unknown versions, non-canonical values, and bounded allocation.

program_gates: Phase-3 public API ADR approval.
Versioned golden and differential corpus.
Shared-file reservation coordination with the bincode surface.
classifications.notes: Bincode and MessagePack work share typed_symbol.rs and must not be executed as conflicting parallel edits.

classifications.replacement_surface: Owned versioned configuration schemas and migration logic over retained serde_json.

public-api-redesign: The config ADR and API surface map record removed TOML/YAML inputs and the chosen versioned JSON contract.
persistent-format: A reviewed migration corpus proves field preservation, unknown-version rejection, and explicit legacy-file disposition.

program_gates: Owner-approved TOML and YAML to versioned JSON migration ADR.
Data-preservation inventory, semantic goldens, and manual corpus review.
Explicit owner permission before deleting any legacy file.
classifications.notes: This row does not approve a home-grown TOML or YAML parser.

classifications.replacement_surface: Owned parser for the complete documented Asupersync CLI contract.

public-api-redesign: The CLI ADR, API surface map, help/error goldens, OsString cases, and exit-code matrix cover the complete consumer contract.

program_gates: Phase-3 CLI API ADR approval.
OsString and invalid-UTF-8 coverage, help and error goldens, and exit-code parity.
Full capability inventory across every clap-consuming file.
classifications.notes: SAFE-OWN does not reduce the XL implementation scope or permit a partial parser.

classifications.replacement_surface: Fixed PII redaction scanners and an owned subset matcher.

security-sensitive-parser: Independent redaction review and adversarial bypass corpus prove unsupported syntax fails closed without silently exposing PII.
runtime-hot-path: Representative log and trace scanning benchmarks bound throughput, allocations, and worst-case input behavior.

program_gates: Unsupported syntax fails closed.
Differential corpus covers every currently accepted pattern.
PII redaction must never silently degrade.
classifications.notes: The production mod-regex mock is not an acceptable replacement.

classifications.replacement_surface: NKey base32 no-pad, CRC-16/XMODEM, and prefix codec over retained ed25519-dalek.

persistent-format: Persisted seed and public-key fixtures retain canonical text encoding and explicit migration or rejection behavior.
wire-format-parser: NATS reference vectors cover every approved prefix, compound seeds, CRC failures, truncation, and non-canonical base32.
security-sensitive-parser: Independent review verifies signer-kind allow matrices, secret redaction, resource bounds, and fail-closed unknown prefixes.
cryptographic-format: Reference vectors and security sign-off cover key-kind type separation while ed25519-dalek remains the primitive.

program_gates: Security review before cutover.
NATS fixture and differential vectors against nkeys as a dev-only oracle.
Marginal-cost ledger baseline and after measurement.
classifications.notes: The codec replacement does not replace or reimplement ed25519-dalek.

classifications.replacement_surface: Finite owned ProtoMessage types and protobuf wire codec.

public-api-redesign: The ProstCodec ADR and API surface map define the finite owned message set and removal of arbitrary prost::Message.
wire-format-parser: Protobuf differential, truncation, varint overflow, recursion, unknown-field, and allocation-bound fixtures cover the finite decoder.
security-sensitive-parser: Independent review verifies hostile-message bounds, ambiguity handling, and deterministic fail-closed errors.

program_gates: Phase-3 ProstCodec public API ADR approval.
Differential proptest coverage against prost as a dev-only oracle.
OTLP and gRPC golden vectors for the finite message inventory.
classifications.notes: The class does not approve retaining an arbitrary prost::Message public surface.

classifications.replacement_surface: Owned exporter traits and native OTLP export over the finite owned protobuf surface.

public-api-redesign: The OTLP ADR and API surface map explicitly approve removal of external Meter and SDK interoperability.
wire-format-parser: OTLP goldens and bounded malformed-response fixtures cover the finite owned protobuf and transport surface.

program_gates: Explicit ADR approval to remove external OpenTelemetry Meter and SDK interoperability.
Owned protobuf prerequisite complete.
Wire-format and collector interoperability evidence.
classifications.notes: Safety eligibility does not approve the interoperability reduction.

classifications.replacement_surface: Owned UtcTimestamp, RFC3339, and serde implementations.

persistent-format: Timestamp formatting and parsing goldens cover stored evidence, trace, report, and migration expectations.

program_gates: Complete call-site inventory.
Differential timestamp, range, and serialization vectors.
classifications.notes: The scope is the finite current timestamp surface, not a general calendar library.

classifications.replacement_surface: Owned sync/oslock.rs wrapper over std::sync Mutex, RwLock, and Condvar with owned guard types.

concurrency-liveness: Deterministic cancellation, wake, poisoning, guard-drop, condvar, and quiescence schedules preserve synchronization semantics.
runtime-hot-path: The agreed 1, 8, 32, and 64-core contention matrix must meet the owner-approved latency and throughput gates.

program_gates: Stable lane remains supported.
Full-axis performance gate across 1, 8, 32, and 64 cores.
Optional nightly nonpoison backend must remain SAFE-OWN and feature-gated.
Owner acceptance for every measured regression.
classifications.notes: A future raw futex or parking backend is not covered by this SAFE-OWN row and is classified separately as ALGORITHMIC-UNSAFE.

classifications.replacement_surface: Owned finite LZ4 block codec.

persistent-format: Historical trace-compression fixtures retain canonical decode or carry an explicit versioned rejection and migration policy.
wire-format-parser: Differential and fuzz fixtures cover malformed blocks, offsets, lengths, truncation, and non-canonical frames.
security-sensitive-parser: Independent review verifies output-size, ratio, allocation, and CPU bounds for hostile compressed input.

program_gates: Differential round-trip and malformed-input corpus.
Performance and compression-ratio gate.
classifications.notes: Any later SIMD acceleration must cite the separate simd-dispatch-boundary row.

classifications.replacement_surface: Owned finite DEFLATE codec for the retained interoperability surface.

wire-format-parser: RFC and differential fixtures cover headers, Huffman tables, block boundaries, truncation, checksums, and rejected malformed streams.
security-sensitive-parser: Independent review verifies decompressed-size, ratio, allocation, and CPU bounds for hostile HTTP content.

program_gates: Format interoperability and malformed-input corpus.
Performance, ratio, and memory-use gate.
Explicit scope decision for compression capabilities.
classifications.notes: This stretch row remains SAFE-OWN; unsafe acceleration requires a different classification.

classifications.replacement_surface: Migration of ATP reader and writer types from the public futures_lite::Stream trait to the blessed owned crate::stream::Stream contract.

public-api-redesign: The futures-lite exit ADR and API surface map name every public ATP Stream implementation and consumer migration.
concurrency-liveness: Deterministic stream polling covers pending, wake, EOF, cancellation, drop, and region-quiescence behavior.
runtime-hot-path: ATP stream throughput, wake count, allocation, and latency are measured before and after migration.

program_gates: Phase-3 futures-lite public Stream ADR approval.
No compatibility shim or second executor semantics.
Public API map and downstream consumer evidence.
classifications.notes: SAFE-OWN does not approve the trait-path break or claim ecosystem interoperability parity.

classifications.replacement_surface: Finite ATP protocol-version scanner and comparator for the exact accepted version grammar.

wire-format-parser: Canonical, overflow, leading-zero, prerelease, truncation, separator, and unsupported-version fixtures cover the exact ATP grammar.
security-sensitive-parser: Independent review verifies downgrade resistance, bounded input, deterministic comparison, and fail-closed unsupported versions.

program_gates: Complete live-call-site grammar inventory.
Differential fixtures against semver for the accepted subset.
Protocol compatibility and downgrade tests.
classifications.notes: The finite scanner must reject general semver syntax that ATP does not explicitly support.

classifications.replacement_surface: Safe mutex-backed queue prototype used only to decide whether a first-party safe replacement can meet the scheduler workload gate.

concurrency-liveness: LabRuntime and stress schedules cover multi-producer ordering, wakeups, cancellation, close, fairness, and no-task-leak behavior.
runtime-hot-path: The owner-approved scheduler matrix compares latency, throughput, contention, fairness, and memory at 1, 8, 32, and 64 cores.

program_gates: Prototype must remain safe Rust and isolated from production until the full gate passes.
KEEP crossbeam-queue if the safe prototype does not win or tie the agreed workload gate.
No result authorizes a first-party lock-free implementation.
classifications.notes: This SAFE-OWN experiment is distinct from the prohibited lock-free-queue candidate.

classifications.replacement_surface: Safe first-party cache-line padding wrapper evaluated against the actual scheduler and synchronization layouts.

platform-boundary: The alignment and size policy names supported architectures, cache-line assumptions, layout checks, and untested targets.
runtime-hot-path: False-sharing, memory-footprint, latency, and throughput measurements cover the actual padded fields on agreed hosts.

program_gates: Layout assertions and architecture matrix.
Before-and-after false-sharing and memory-footprint evidence.
KEEP the incumbent unless the safe wrapper passes the agreed full-axis gate.
classifications.notes: A safe repr(align) wrapper remains SAFE-OWN even though its review obligations are platform-sensitive.

classifications.replacement_surface: Cross-platform reactor boundary covering epoll, kqueue, IOCP, wakeups, fd reuse, generation tokens, and registration races.

concurrency-liveness: Deterministic registration, readiness, wake, cancellation, close, and stale-token schedules preserve reactor progress.
platform-boundary: Linux, macOS, and Windows ABI and host evidence name every unexercised backend and platform limitation.
runtime-hot-path: Real reactor workloads compare syscall rate, wake latency, throughput, contention, and allocation behavior.

program_gates: Measured defect or suite-wide platform-boundary project.
Cross-platform conformance evidence.
Explicit owner sign-off.
classifications.notes: The current marginal cost is about one package; taxonomy eligibility is not a recommendation to replace polling.

classifications.replacement_surface: Cross-platform socket creation, SockAddr conversion, abstract Unix addresses, SockRef, keepalive, and option handling.

platform-boundary: Per-OS socket option, address, handle ownership, inheritance, and error-mapping evidence names untested targets.
runtime-hot-path: Connection, accept, datagram, and option-setting workloads compare latency, syscall count, and allocation behavior.

program_gates: Measured defect or suite-wide platform-boundary project.
Cross-platform socket conformance evidence.
Explicit owner sign-off.
classifications.notes: The current marginal cost is about one package; taxonomy eligibility is not a recommendation to replace socket2.

classifications.replacement_surface: Owned cross-platform signal registration and process-signal boundary.

concurrency-liveness: Deterministic coalescing, registration, wake, cancellation, teardown, and repeated-signal schedules preserve progress.
platform-boundary: Unix and Windows signal or control-handler ABI, async-signal-safety, and untested-host evidence are explicit.

program_gates: Measured performance, maintenance, or idiom-unification justification.
Cross-platform process lifecycle evidence.
Explicit owner sign-off.
classifications.notes: signal-hook compiles no C in the current feature set; native-code removal is not a valid standalone claim.

classifications.replacement_surface: Owned host and process resource probes over procfs, sysctl, and Windows APIs.

platform-boundary: Linux procfs or sysfs, macOS sysctl, and Windows API fixtures distinguish missing, denied, malformed, and unsupported data.

program_gates: Measured generality, performance, or maintenance defect.
Resource-monitor hot-path performance gate.
Cross-platform focused evidence and explicit owner sign-off.
classifications.notes: The signal kill path must be coordinated with signal-platform rather than duplicated.

classifications.replacement_surface: Owned Unix extended-attribute boundary preserving dereference and no-dereference semantics plus macOS signatures.

platform-boundary: Linux and macOS ABI signatures, dereference policy, namespace behavior, error mapping, and untested targets are explicit.

program_gates: Final-rustix-parent trigger or a broader platform-boundary project.
Per-platform xattr round-trip evidence.
Explicit owner sign-off.
classifications.notes: Adding unsafe solely to remove one small pure-Rust crate is not approved by this row.

classifications.replacement_surface: Strict DER residue for SPKI extraction and SAN access that cannot be delegated to rustls or webpki.

wire-format-parser: Strict DER fixtures cover lengths, tags, nesting, truncation, canonical encodings, trailing bytes, and delegation boundaries.
security-sensitive-parser: Independent X.509 review verifies ambiguity rejection, resource bounds, name, time, and extension handling, and delegate-to-webpki-first scope.

program_gates: Delegate-to-webpki inventory complete.
Differential fuzz corpus and independent security review.
Full-input consumption, DER canonicality, depth, duplicate-extension, and fail-closed ambiguity proofs.
Explicit owner sign-off.
classifications.notes: Strict safe-Rust DER parsing remains SAFE-OWN; parser sensitivity is represented only by the orthogonal review tags. This row never approves first-party certificate chain validation, name constraints, wildcards, or path building.

classifications.replacement_surface: Target-feature dispatch shims for an otherwise safe owned codec or numerical kernel.

platform-boundary: Target-feature detection, architecture fallbacks, Miri limitations, and exercised CPU or host combinations are explicit.
runtime-hot-path: Scalar and accelerated kernels are benchmarked and differentially checked across aligned, unaligned, small, and large inputs.

program_gates: Safe scalar implementation exists and remains the parity oracle.
Architecture-specific benchmark demonstrates the need for unsafe acceleration.
Codegen and target-feature gate review.
classifications.notes: The boundary class applies only to dispatch and intrinsics, not to unsafe ownership or initialization tricks inside the kernel.

classifications.replacement_surface: First-party lock-free queue using pointer tagging, epoch ownership, or manual reclamation.

concurrency-liveness: Any exception requires a concrete linearizability, reclamation, ABA, cancellation, fairness, and liveness proof plan.
runtime-hot-path: The safe mutex-backed prototype must first fail the agreed real-workload gate on the same axes.

program_gates: Benchmark a SAFE-OWN mutex-backed alternative first.
Retain the incumbent unless the complete exception process is approved.
classifications.notes: The incumbent's mature, widely-fuzzed algorithmic unsafe is part of its value.

classifications.replacement_surface: First-party inline storage using MaybeUninit or manual drop and initialization tracking.

runtime-hot-path: Any exception requires a measured allocation or locality defect and a safe inline or heap-backed alternative benchmark.

program_gates: Retain smallvec under the default policy.
Do not treat a future nonzero marginal as safety approval.
classifications.notes: Marginal dependency savings cannot change the safety class.

classifications.replacement_surface: Generated first-party pin-projection machinery.

concurrency-liveness: Any exception requires a soundness plan for structural pinning, conditional Unpin, drop, cancellation, and generator state.
runtime-hot-path: Any exception requires a measured macro or runtime defect and benchmarked safe or retained alternatives.

program_gates: Retain pin-project under the default policy.
A suite-wide macro consolidation still requires the complete exception process.
classifications.notes: The small marginal reduction does not justify fresh soundness-critical macro code.

classifications.replacement_surface: First-party futex, WaitOnAddress, or os_unfair_lock parking protocol behind sync/oslock.rs.

concurrency-liveness: Any exception requires a waiter-ownership, wake-loss, fairness, cancellation, poisoning, and quiescence proof plan.
platform-boundary: Any exception requires separate futex, WaitOnAddress, and os_unfair_lock ABI and memory-order evidence.
runtime-hot-path: The safe std::sync backend must first fail the agreed 1, 8, 32, and 64-core real-workload gates.

program_gates: The SAFE-OWN std::sync wrapper must be implemented and benchmarked first.
Retain the safe backend unless the complete exception process is approved.
classifications.notes: The optional nightly nonpoison backend in parking-lot-wrapper is not this raw-lock fallback and must remain safe.

explicit_no_claims: This citation does not prove marginal dependency savings or broad workspace health.

validation.command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_DEBUG=0 RUSTFLAGS='-D warnings -C debuginfo=0' CARGO_TARGET_DIR="${RCH_TARGET_BASE:-${TMPDIR:-/tmp}}/rch_target_dependency_safety_taxonomy" cargo test -p asupersync --test dependency_safety_taxonomy_contract -- --nocapture
